description: Diff readout produced by running the Constitution string through the skill-cluster parse with left originalist nodes and right modern nodes.

outcome_1.label: Original meaning from 1789/1913 dictionary evidence and constitutional text
outcome_1.summary: The Constitution, read in its original public meaning, limits legislative power to the law-making body; restricts commerce to trade/exchange; reserves coinage and monetary standard to Congress; protects the writ of habeas corpus except in rebellion or invasion; defines treason narrowly with procedural safeguards; and bars impairment of contract obligations and receipt of emoluments without consent.
outcome_1.drift_method: now_vs_then from local Gutenberg Webster sections + immutable constitutional text

outcome_2.label: Modern legal meaning from statutory/administrative structures
outcome_2.summary: Modern legal structures accept broad delegation of legislative power, expansive Commerce Clause authority, fiat/electronic money systems, narrowed habeas and writ substitutes, functional statutory replacements for treason, and contested contract/emolument boundaries. The drift is not uniform: some terms remain close to original meaning, while others are inverted or expanded.
outcome_2.drift_method: modern statutory/administrative mapping with circuit-split annotations where contested

diff_summary.originalist: Legislative power is non-delegable by original meaning.
diff_summary.modern: Delegation to agencies is broadly accepted; nondelegation is nominal.
diff_summary.blast_radius: All agency rulemaking, administrative procedure, and regulatory state structures.

diff_summary.originalist: Commerce is trade/exchange between persons or states.
diff_summary.modern: Substantial-effect doctrine covers nearly all economic activity.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Federal preemption of state licensing, professional regulation, and local economic law.

diff_summary.originalist: Direct taxes require apportionment; indirect taxes require uniformity.
diff_summary.modern: Income tax and administrative levies depart from apportionment.
diff_summary.blast_radius: All federal revenue-collection structures outside the original tax formula.

diff_summary.originalist: Congress alone mints coin; states prohibited.
diff_summary.modern: Federal Reserve system delegates monetary base management; state coinage remains barred.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Monetary standard, central banking, CBDC/stablecoin proposals.

diff_summary.originalist: States may make only gold/silver tender; federal baseline is specie-based.
diff_summary.modern: Federal fiat currency is legal tender; states cannot contravene.
diff_summary.blast_radius: State-backed currency initiatives, gold clause revival, payment-system design.

diff_summary.originalist: Citizen body for local defense, separate from standing army.
diff_summary.modern: National Guard is federalized; selective service exists; citizen militia is not functional.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Federal-state military authority division, conscription, state defense forces.

diff_summary.originalist: Legislative punishment without judicial trial is barred.
diff_summary.modern: Civil contempt, regulatory disqualification, and registration regimes act as functional equivalents.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Civil litigation, administrative penalties, firearm/sex-offender registries.

diff_summary.modern: Electronic reserves, FR notes, stablecoins, CBDCs are money.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Payment systems, central-bank digital currency, reserve accounting.

diff_summary.originalist: Judicial written order; habeas corpus is the preserved example.
diff_summary.modern: Administrative subpoenas, NSLs, geofence warrants, secret FISA orders substitute for writs.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Surveillance, administrative process, secret court procedures.

diff_summary.originalist: Narrow definition: levying war or adhering to enemies; two-witness or confession rule.
diff_summary.modern: Same label rare; adjacent statutes carry similar penalties without the same safeguards.
diff_summary.blast_radius: National-security prosecutions, material-support statutes, espionage charges.

diff_summary.originalist: Great Writ; produce the body before a judge.
diff_summary.modern: Available but narrowed by military commissions, indefinite-detention statutes, and suspension debates.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Detention policy, military-commission review, Guantanamo-style litigation.

diff_summary.originalist: No retroactive criminal law or punishment enhancement.
diff_summary.modern: Civil/regulatory retroactivity is contested; SORNA-like application survived in part.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Retroactive civil penalties, registration laws, regulatory enforcement timing.

diff_summary.originalist: States may not impair the obligation of contracts.
diff_summary.modern: Emergency controls, bankruptcy, moratoria, and consent decrees modify obligations.
diff_summary.blast_radius: State emergency powers, mortgage/foreclosure law, consent decrees.

diff_summary.originalist: Officeholders may not receive foreign/domestic emoluments without consent.
diff_summary.modern: Business interests, OLC narrowing, and enforcement mechanisms are contested.
diff_summary.blast_radius: Presidential business holdings, foreign-payment channels, ethics enforcement.

conclusion.originalist_outcome: The Constitution's original meaning creates a tightly constrained federal structure with explicit anti-delegation, anti-emolument, monetary-specie, judicial-writ, and narrow-treason protections.
conclusion.modern_outcome: Modern legal meaning accepts or tolerates substantial departures from those constraints, producing a document whose text remains while its operative meaning has shifted.
conclusion.final_diff: 14 terms compared. 9 RED: meaning inverted or structurally departed. 5 ORANGE: meaning expanded, substituted, or functionally bypassed. English crown lineage remains excluded from both readouts.
